title: Spreadsheet Basics
subtitle: learn tables, formulas & calculations!
overview: This session introduces students to spreadsheets using Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets. Students learn rows, columns, cells, formulas, calculations, and how spreadsheets help organize and analyze data easily.

images._note: Add classroom screenshots, spreadsheet examples, student activities, charts, and formula demonstrations.

activity.title: Create a Student Marksheet
steps: Open Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel
Create columns: Name, English, Maths, Science
Use SUM formula to calculate total marks
Use AVERAGE formula to calculate average
Format the table with colors and borders
Create a bar chart using the marks data

quiz.q: What is a Spreadsheet mainly used for?
quiz.explanation: Spreadsheets help organize, calculate, and analyze data easily.

quiz.q: A Spreadsheet is made up of:
quiz.explanation: Spreadsheets contain rows and columns that form cells.

quiz.q: What is the intersection of a row and column called?
quiz.explanation: A cell is formed where a row and column meet.

quiz.q: Which of the following is a cell reference?
quiz.explanation: A1 is a cell reference where A is the column and 1 is the row.

quiz.q: Which symbol is used to start a formula in spreadsheets?
quiz.explanation: All spreadsheet formulas begin with the '=' symbol.
The SUM function is used to add numbers automatically.

options: Adds values from A1 and B1
quiz.explanation: The formula adds the values stored in cells A1 and B1.

quiz.q: Which software can be used to create spreadsheets?
quiz.explanation: Google Sheets is a spreadsheet application.

quiz.q: What is used to show data visually in a spreadsheet?
quiz.explanation: Charts help represent data visually using bars, lines, or pie graphs.

quiz.q: Which function calculates the average of numbers?
quiz.explanation: AVERAGE calculates the mean value of selected numbers.

quiz.q: Rows in spreadsheets are identified by:
Columns in spreadsheets are identified by:
quiz.explanation: Columns are labeled using letters like A, B, C, etc.

quiz.q: What happens when you change a value used in a formula?
quiz.explanation: Spreadsheet formulas recalculate automatically when data changes.

quiz.q: Which feature helps make tables colorful and neat?
quiz.explanation: Formatting helps improve the appearance of spreadsheet data.

quiz.q: Why are spreadsheets useful for schools?
options: To create marksheets and attendance records
quiz.explanation: Schools use spreadsheets for marksheets, attendance, calculations, and reports.
